title: potentilla cinerea subvar. incana
intro: Potentilla cinerea subvar. incana, also known as the grey-leaved cinquefoil, is a variety of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae), characterized by its greyish-green leaves and delicate white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to soothe skin irritations and protect against cell damage. The health benefits of Potentilla cinerea subvar. incana are believed to include reduced inflammation, improved skin health, and enhanced immune function, making it a valuable herb in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve drying and infusing the leaves in hot water or oil to create a tea or tincture that can be used to treat various health conditions.
